entries.title: First three-factor Cox ledger
entries.scope: For three ordered linear factors and the irreducible boundary p0+p3=0, the normalized complement has unit rank one and trivial Picard group. One primitive coordinate gives an integrally unimodular full-boundary ledger and a constant-Jacobian finite etale degree-six morphism between smooth affine fourfolds. The source retains its nonconstant unit and is not affine four-space; no affine-space or permutation-reduction claim is made.

entries.title: All-arity linear-factor Cox ledgers
entries.scope: For every s>=3 ordered linear binary factors, the full boundary lattice has free unit rank (s-1)(s-2)/2 and Smith index one for odd s and two for even s. A minimal-index normalization tree gives an exact ambient determinant factorization and a finite etale Cox--Kummer factorization cover. One primitive coordinate per independent boundary character gives a constant-Jacobian separated suspension attaining the unit rank. A one-coordinate compressed suspension has the same property, disproving the unrestricted extra-dimension lower bound. Neither construction extends across collision divisors or gives an affine-space or permutation map.

entries.title: Oriented cubic Cox chart
entries.scope: For three ordered linear factors, allowing r12=0 and adjoining the oriented discriminant coordinate D to the target gives a polynomial etale morphism between smooth affine principal Cox/discriminant charts. Its hypersurface-residue Jacobian is the constant -1/2 and its generic degree is three. One collision branch remains affine while the other two give distinct dicritical divisors over D=0. The full normalized source has class L^3-2L-[Spec k[x]/(x^2-x+1)] and is not affine three-space over C. Both dicriticals share one target image, and finite-field permutation behavior still fails.

entries.title: Toric Cox--Keller permutation maps
entries.scope: For arbitrary exponents n_i>=2, explicit separated and compressed power-cover suspensions on (G_m)^r times affine space have determinant one and geometric degree product n_i. Their reductions over F_q are permutations exactly when gcd(n_i,q-1)=1 for every i; odd exponents therefore give infinitely many permutation prime fields. This proves compatibility of nontrivial Cox--Keller covers with permutation reductions, but has abelian toric monodromy and does not supply the collision/dicritical geometry of the factorization charts.

entries.title: Affine descent of the oriented cubic Cox chart
entries.scope: For the tangent hyperplane p1=1, quotienting the oriented three-linear-factor chart by swapping L1 and L2 gives the normalized linear--quadratic factorization slice A^3, while quotienting the oriented target by D -> -D gives coefficient A^3. The descended multiplication map has determinant -1 and is linearly equivalent to the foundational cubic Keller map. The two distinct Cox dicritical divisors are exchanged by the involution and merge into one affine boundary prime; permutation monodromy is not obtained.

entries.title: All-arity oriented Cox maps
entries.scope: For every s>=3 ordered linear factors and a minimal-index normalization tree, adjoining the Vandermonde orientation D to the coefficient target gives a polynomial etale morphism on smooth Cox--Kummer charts with constant hypersurface-residue Jacobian +/-d_s/2 and generic degree d_s*s!/2. Every non-tree collision extends as an affine divisor, while the s-1 tree collision types give distinct dicritical boundary primes over the oriented discriminant. This supplies arbitrarily many dicritical primes on Cox charts. Retaining two after affine-space descent and combining them with permutation monodromy remain open.

entries.title: Oriented Davenport Cox maps
entries.scope: For each degree-seven Davenport point/line cover with pullback boundary E3*E6*J^2, adjoining W^2=E3*E6 on the source and D^2=Delta on the common target gives a polynomial finite etale degree-seven Cox morphism via D=JW. The hypersurface-residue Jacobian is one and the oriented boundary ledger is reduced to three components. The common GL_3(F_2) Galois closure and identical point/line fiber zeta functions persist. Fixed-point counts {0,1,3,7} prevent a global permutation family, and the charts are not affine spaces.

entries.title: Oriented cubic quotient rigidity
entries.scope: For the tangent slice r13=r23=p1=1, the oriented source has Grothendieck class L^3+L-1 and is not affine or stably affine over C. The pointwise stabilizer in S3 of its two dicritical collision edges is trivial, while their setwise stabilizer is generated by the transposition swapping them. Hence every nontrivial factor-permutation quotient preserving the dicritical pair merges it. Adding anti-invariant linear suspension coordinates makes the local involution quotient singular, while invariant coordinates leave the merger unchanged; a nonsymmetric affine modification remains open.

entries.title: Davenport exceptional-twist obstruction
entries.scope: The natural degree-seven GL_3(F_2) subgroup is self-normalizing in S_7. Hence any arithmetic monodromy group with this normal geometric point or line action equals the geometric group, leaving no nontrivial Frobenius coset and no exceptional twist. The fixed-point counts are {0,1,3,7}. Permutation reductions therefore require changing the degree, geometric group, representation, or generic monodromy.

entries.title: Classification of linear coefficient hyperplanes
entries.scope: Over an algebraically closed characteristic-zero field, every nonzero linear functional on binary-cubic coefficients is, under normalized factor changes, of triple-root, double-root, or squarefree dual-cubic type. The corresponding three-factor Cox sources have classes L^3-2L^2+L, L^3+L-1, and L^3-2L-2. None is affine or becomes affine after ordinary stabilization. Thus every successful affine modification must be nonlinear in coefficient space or change the boundary normalization.

entries.title: Homogeneous coefficient-hypersurface Cox obstruction
entries.scope: For a homogeneous degree-d polynomial F in the binary-cubic coefficients, the normalization rows r13,r23,F have Smith form diag(1,1,d). If d>1, the level source carries a free residual mu_d action, so compactly supported Euler-characteristic divisibility excludes affine space and every ordinary affine stabilization. Degree one is already excluded by the complete linear-hyperplane classification. Thus no homogeneous coefficient hypersurface yields an affine normalized three-factor source.

entries.title: Cox vector-bundle stabilization obstruction
entries.scope: For every degree-one three-factor coefficient slice, the Hodge defect remains after every Zariski-locally trivial affine-space or vector-bundle extension. For every higher homogeneous degree, any mu_d-equivariant vector-bundle extension with arbitrary linear fiber characters retains the free residual action, and Euler-characteristic divisibility excludes affine total space. Primitive Cox coordinates can evade the obstruction only by entering a genuinely nonlinear modification rather than a vector-bundle suspension.

entries.title: Triple-root affine completion obstruction
entries.scope: The triple-root slice r13=r23=p0=1 is G_m^2 times A^1 and has the evident A^3 completion with two distinct missing torus primes. Minimal monomial clearing of p1,p2,p3 and the oriented discriminant gives a polynomial map to an A^3 target, but its determinant is -6abc(bc+1)(a^2c-bc-1). The three extra affine factors cannot be absorbed by target reparametrization because the cleared target has only constant units.

entries.title: Affine-source triple-root Cox map
entries.scope: Orienting the five-factor determinant of the minimally cleared triple-root chart gives a polynomial map from A^3 to the hypersurface 3R^2=4C(1-E)(A-E-1)(A-E+2)(A+2E-1). On smooth principal charts its residue Jacobian is 1/6. The map is birational with an explicit inverse; one branch extends finitely and four distinct target components are dicritical. Over every finite field of characteristic greater than three, source and target both have q^3 points, but the exact fiber sizes 0,1,q-1,q,2q-1 rule out permutation behavior.

entries.title: Primitive-multiplicity triple-root Cox map
entries.scope: The triple-root affine source maps birationally to u^2v=xy(x+1)(x-y+1) by x=bc, y=a^2c, u=ac, v=b(x+1)(x-y+1). Its hypersurface-residue Jacobian is -1. One boundary component extends finitely and three have distinct dicritical target images. Exhaustion of all monomial divisors of the pulled-back four-line product proves u=ac with multiplicity two is the unique constant-residue factor-splitting target. The target has q(q-1)(q+4) finite-field points, so it is not a permutation model.

entries.title: Triple-root affine-modification chain
entries.scope: The square-oriented target S^2=Cxy(x+1)(x-y+1), the primitive target u^2v=xy(x+1)(x-y+1), and three further strict transforms form one residue-crepant affine-modification chain obtained by adjoining u=S/C, a=y/u, c=u/a, and b=x/c. Dicritical counts are 4,3,1,1,0. The square stage and final A^3 stage both have q^3 points, but only the final identity stage has bijective fibers; singularities and intermediate point-count excess disappear only as all dicritical components are filled or moved.

entries.title: Smooth Danielewski triple-root map
entries.scope: The balanced modification w=x(x+1)/c pulls back polynomially as b(bc+1) and gives a map A^3 -> {cw=x(x+1)} times A^1 with residue Jacobian -1. The target is globally smooth, has class L^3+L^2 and q^3+q^2 finite-field points, and retains exactly one dicritical component. For last-step modifications g(x)/c, source polynomiality forces g(0)=0, while retaining the x=-1 center forces g(-1)=0; the minimal balanced target is therefore Danielewski, whereas the affine-space choice x/c fills the dicritical.

entries.title: Danielewski multi-dicritical maps with bijective reductions
entries.scope: For every squarefree P=xQ, the map (a,b,c)->(a,c,bc,bQ(bc)) is a residue-Jacobian -1 open immersion from A^3 into the smooth target cw=P(x). Its complement is the disjoint union of one A^2 dicritical divisor for every nonzero geometric root of P, so the target class is L^3+(deg(P)-1)L^2. Over F_q the map is bijective on rational points exactly when 0 is the only rational root. For P=x(x^2+1), two geometric dicriticals persist and the reductions are bijective for every prime p congruent to 3 mod 4. The target is not A^3.

entries.title: Danielewski Poisson-contraction obstruction
entries.scope: For D_P={cw=P(x)}, an a-preserving contraction produces an ordinary determinant -1 map A^3->A^3 exactly when two Danielewski coordinates have intrinsic bracket one. If P is squarefree of degree greater than one, the residue form has nonzero period 2*pi*i*(beta-alpha) on the vanishing sphere joining any two roots, so no polynomial pair has nonzero constant bracket. More generally the full three-coordinate equation is A_a{F,G}-F_a{A,G}+G_a{A,F}=1; the period obstruction shows that any solution must make at least two target coordinates depend nontrivially on a. The direct affine-linear critical-point, slice, and grading proof is retained as an algebraic cross-check.

entries.title: Minimal coupled Danielewski ansatz obstruction
entries.scope: For P=x(x^2+1), Cauchy--Binet gives [x^2]J=3[1]J on span(1,a,c,x,w,ac,ax,aw), excluding the smallest genuinely coupled ansatz. Adjoining all diagonal ambient quadratics a^2,c^2,x^2,w^2,cw gives the stronger lock [x^2]J-[x^4]J+[x^6]J=3[1]J. The normalized five-equation certificate for the cx,xw escape chart omits the three residual target coefficients of x, so that final chart conclusion is only proved on the zero-linear-x slice.

entries.title: Quadratic Danielewski interaction support bound
entries.scope: On the normalized slice with zero residual linear-x coefficients, allow the common directions ac,ax,aw and partition the remaining directions into diagonals D={a^2,c^2,x^2,w^2,cw} and escapes E={cx,xw}. Exact coefficient-ideal calculations over Q give unit Groebner bases for all 68 charts S with 1<=|S|<=4 and S meeting E. The later audit shows that normalization on (a,c,w) does not itself remove the three target coefficients of x, so this support bound is a slice statement.

entries.title: Coupled Danielewski leading-face and flux reduction
entries.scope: For every solution A,F,G of A_a{F,G}-F_a{A,G}+G_a{A,F}=1, let r,s,t be the a-degrees and choose r>0. The highest a-coefficient forces the weighted ratios F_s^r/A_r^s and G_t^r/A_r^t to Poisson-commute and hence be algebraically dependent in the Danielewski function field. Independently, the relative de Rham class satisfies d/da[A d_DF wedge d_DG]=[omega], so its flux is exactly a[omega]+constant and all higher a-coefficients vanish in H^2. These identities are exact, but the all-degree descent remains open and a normalized cubic family shows that leading collapse plus correct flux alone is compatible.

entries.title: Cubic Danielewski de Rham compiler
entries.scope: For D={cw=P(x)}, top de Rham cohomology is the Poisson-commutator quotient B/{B,B}, canonically represented by k[x]/d(Pk[x]). For P=x^3+x this has basis [1],[x] and recurrence [x^(m+2)]=-(m+1)/(m+3)[x^m]. The resulting exact compiler sends the coupled flux A{F,G} to two polynomial coordinates whose derivative must be (1,0). For the corrected normalized maximal ambient-quadratic ansatz, including all three residual linear-x coefficients, the flux compiles to exactly five nonconstant equations, each of total coefficient degree three.

entries.title: Full ambient-quadratic Danielewski obstruction
entries.scope: For P=x(x^2+1), every ambient-quadratic candidate normalizes to (a,c,w) while retaining three residual coefficients of x=bc. The corrected determinant ledger has 33 coefficients; three exact linear eliminations leave 30 variables and 89 equations. Column-major exact modular reconstruction over Q gives the reduced Groebner basis {1}, closing the full ambient-quadratic coefficient scheme in characteristic zero.
supersedes: the zero-linear-x quadratic slice certificate

entries.title: All-degree Danielewski contraction audit
entries.scope: Any coupled solution on A^1 times {cw=x(x^2+1)} composes with the canonical A^3 open immersion to give a noninvertible three-dimensional Keller map. The corrected quadratic ideal is exactly unit over Q. Correct cubic normalization leaves 90 coefficients and the de Rham compiler gives 11 nonconstant flux equations. An explicit normalized cubic family satisfies the leading-face collapse and the complete compiled flux identity while failing J=1 pointwise, so those two gates alone cannot prove the all-degree obstruction. The two boundary roots have equal P'=-2, and one-boundary poles of the leading rational pencil are genuine.

entries.title: Normalized quadratic--cubic factorization slice
entries.scope: The normalized factorization slice has the proved smoothness, factoriality, motivic, Chow, K-theoretic, and homotopy invariants.
supersedes: affine-five-space candidate for the tangent quadratic--cubic slice

entries.title: Plane Jacobian degree frontier
entries.scope: locally reproduced conditional on the published minimal/standard normal-form reductions: any plane counterexample has larger coordinate degree at least 125; this does not prove JC(2) or attainability
dependencies: external: published minimal-standard-pair reductions and exact (72,108) certificates
supersedes: larger-coordinate frontier 108

entries.title: Dicritical boundary classification
entries.scope: proved for every admissible primitive in characteristic zero: all dicritical divisors of the normalized inverse graph and their target images are classified
supersedes: unresolved boundary-at-infinity divisor list

entries.title: Higher cancellation contact resultants
entries.scope: Prove the residual finite staircase 7<=r<=X_m/m in columns m>=1001 not covered by parameter irreducibility; above the Baker--Harman--Pintz threshold it also satisfies r<5(mr)^(21/40). The first six fixed-r columns are complete, and r=7 is proved for all sufficiently large m without an effective threshold. Any unresolved pair has composite N=(m+1)r+1 and at most one prime in (mr,N).

entries.title: Linear target-lift Rees strictness
entries.scope: For every relevant filtered face, construct the target-field modules weight by weight, their lifted initial submodule in h_F, and the normal quotient, and prove that taking the initial face commutes with the linear target lift. Treat this as a finite module/SAGBI problem, not a further target-jet expansion; the same presentation should expose the finite set of weights used by the quadratic calculation.

entries.title: Quadratic target-lift LR symbol classification
entries.scope: The minimal pair p=1 is already nonzero as O8, so quadratic strict descent fails. Compute the remaining generator matrices for II_(F,p,-p), prove a structural finite-weight cutoff, and determine whether the minimal class generates the full quadratic normal image.

entries.title: Marked-cover valuative no escape
entries.scope: Construct the proper closure of the LR-induced Isom relation first on marked normalized incidence covers. Use exact recovery from the marked Hessian divisor and affine sheet, unique coarse specialization at every DVR collision, and generic deck rigidity to prove that this marked Isom object is finite and proper on the clean locus; extend over a Hurwitz/admissible-cover compactification and reconstruct the LR equivalence only afterwards.

entries.title: Hessian-Ritt reduction and degree-six incidence classification
entries.scope: Vertical (a,b)-decomposability is the pullback of the projective Hessian-composition incidence and is finite etale over that incidence on the D1/F2 clean open. Its codimension is at least N-a-b, proving that generic seeds in every composite degree N>=6 have no imprimitive vertical specialization. In degree six the 3-o-2 and 2-o-3 incidences are explicit cubic and quartic hypersurfaces of exact expected codimension one; their intersection is exactly the monomial/Chebyshev Ritt collision, its normalized marked seed curve and clean-boundary exclusions are explicit, and a rational clean witness is given.
supersedes: degree-six full coefficient elimination for OP-RITT

entries.title: Degree-six Ritt affine-sheet boundary atlas
entries.scope: After exact-degree saturation, the degree-six 2-o-3 and 3-o-2 Ritt surfaces meet the affine-sheet reconstruction boundary in respectively two and three irreducible curves, each with a rational Hessian-clean admissible witness. On their common marked Ritt curve the zero-cluster cut is one reduced sextic orbit, the extra-root cut is reduced with rational splitting 1+1+4, and the four remaining Hessian collisions are exactly the type-(6) omitted-component support and are disjoint from the affine boundary.
supersedes: degree-six affine-sheet boundary part of OP-RITT

entries.title: All-degree Hessian atlas and degree-eight/twelve Ritt diagrams
entries.scope: For every factor pair ab=N, canonical monic coefficient reconstruction makes the Hessian-composition incidence rational of exact codimension N-a-b. In degree eight the two factor-order loci meet in the irreducible excess 2-o-2-o-2 refinement locus of marked dimension two. In degree twelve all six pair intersections and all higher intersections are classified: three common-refinement components, two transported degree-six Ritt components, power and Chebyshev components for 3-o-4 versus 4-o-3, and the all-four intersection is exactly the Chebyshev component. Every component meets the D1/F2 clean open.
supersedes: generic-finiteness and expected-codimension part of OP-RITT
degree-eight and degree-twelve Ritt-intersection diagrams

entries.title: Canonical Hessian linear lifts and low-degree global synchronization
entries.scope: For every normalized factor pair ab=N in characteristic zero, triangular reconstruction uses only coefficients of degrees at least two and defines a canonical missing linear coefficient lambda_(a,b) on the Hessian-composition scheme. A multiple intersection is scheme-theoretically synchronized exactly when all lift differences lie in the summed Hessian residual ideal. Exact ambient, canonical-factor-chart, and transported Dickson normal-coordinate Groebner reduction proves every pair, hence every multiple intersection, through degree 24. The final degree-24 pair {2,3} reduces in a 4-normal/5-base block ring with Groebner basis size 63. In degree 30, five exact pair certificates with basis sizes 11,6,95,6,11 form the spanning tree 2-6-3-15-5-10 and prove that the global all-six intersection is scheme-theoretically synchronized. The nested pair {2,10} is separately certified in a 4-normal/7-base ring with basis size 4. Five incomparable degree-30 two-cut subintersections remain uncertified.
supersedes: global synchronization part of OP-RITT through degree 24
global synchronization of the degree-30 all-six intersection

entries.title: Degree-thirty coefficient-decorated Ritt 2-complex
entries.scope: On all six normalized complete-decomposition charts of the filled S3 braid hexagon, the degree permutations share the explicit Dickson A2 coefficient map. A half-braid omitting a prime cut is reduced, while one omitting a composite cut is nonreduced on z=0. For omitted cuts 10,15,6 the respective (nilpotence index, defect annihilator) data are (4,(z^2)), (3,(z^2)), and (4,(z^4)); opposite endpoint charts agree. Exact elimination identifies their annihilator slices as Q[u]/(u^5), Q[u,v]/(u^2,v^2), and Q[u,v]/(u^4,v^2). Their augmentation kernels have lengths 4,3,7; point-cotangent homology ranks are (1,1), (2,2), (2,2); and intrinsic Tor series are 1/(1-T), 1/(1-T)^2, 1/(1-T)^2. The tangent asymmetry survives in ambient polynomial and Hessian coefficient spaces. Restoring every linear residual leaves all path and boundary ideals unchanged on every chart, proving exact local Hessian transfer throughout the complete-decomposition atlas. The independent HRSYNC spanning-tree certificate proves global synchronization of the all-six intersection, but additional synchronized components missing the atlas are not excluded.
supersedes: set-theoretic degree-thirty braid comparison

entries.title: Vertical imprimitive-specialization loci
entries.scope: Prove synchronization for the five uncertified incomparable degree-30 two-cut intersections {2,3}, {2,5}, {2,15}, {3,5}, and {3,10}, and then uniformly in all degrees; prove the reduced Ritt-complex component conjecture; exclude or classify additional synchronized degree-30 all-cut components missing the complete-decomposition atlas; glue the now-explicit degree-30 transverse augmentations and cotangent/Tor signatures along the monomial divisor; organize analogous coherence data on commuting and mixed power-Chebyshev cells; and extend the degree-six affine-sheet boundary atlas to later incidence components.

entries.title: Restricted cubic and quartic-HN exact minima
entries.scope: Determine the exact values in the current intervals 3<=r_cub<=17, 3<=nu_cub<=18, 3<=rho_HN,4<=37, and 6<=n_HN,4<=42. The rank/index inequality nu<=rank+1 makes the full-class index program the master lower-bound attack. The proposed index-three inverse-degree bound nine is false: van den Essen's exact dimension-five cubic automorphism has generic rank three, weak index three, and inverse degree thirteen. Its degree-eleven term realizes Omega_11=-C(NH,H,H)/2-B(B(H,H),H)/2-N(C(H,H,H))/6 nontrivially, so the class is not killed by the full coefficient ideal of (JH)^3=0; the invertibility-only full-class theorem remains open. In the four-variable lambda plus two arbitrary binary-cubic normal-form family, exact coefficient extraction proves that index three forces lambda=0, rank at most two, and inverse degree at most five; every rank-three member has index at least four. On the quartic side, the exact cotangent formula rank Hess(y.H)=2 rank(JH)+rank(A restricted to ker JH) reduces the next upper-bound target to rank 18 with zero kernel excess or rank 17 with excess at most two. The current rank-37 source has exact generic excess one; the rank-17 source has sampled excess four. In the combined 140-terminal search the rank-37 source is uniquely excess one and no terminal has sampled excess zero. Continue with the remaining rank-three kernel-bundle strata, excess-directed circuit synthesis, and direct dimension-six HN saturation.
dependencies: external: fixed-rank and low-dimensional positive results

entries.title: Absolute global Sunada Keller maps
entries.scope: Replace the Cox-Keller boundary-complement and relative affine-bundle realizations by two absolute polynomial Keller maps over one affine-space target. AS2 rules out stable straightening and coordinate-preserving suspension; AS3-AS5 exhaust the fixed-mark and standard weighted-gluing routes. AS6 matches the doubled modification Jacobian with J^2 but finds a center unit-rank mismatch. AS7 constructs the unique puncture-merging quotient on the normalization and isolates its node-conductor failure. AS8 separates that conductor in affine planes, finds the unique torus-clean parabola chart with exact doubled derivative pullback, proves that its complementary gluing creates a complete exceptional P1, and excludes every polynomial-coordinate one-chart separation. AS9-AS12 eliminate the direct determinant splice, exposed exceptional parameter, affine and canonical nonlinear masks, and the unshifted determinant output. AS13 normalizes the translated incidence Sigma=S+D^2U-CR. AS14-AS15 eliminate coefficient dependence through T, Y, or T+Y^2. AS16 gives the invariant unit gate for arbitrary coefficient pencils, and AS17 uses it to eliminate both one-triangular Jung families, including the exceptional quadratic coefficient ideal. The leading unresolved gate is therefore a translated incidence whose affine-in-U coefficient coordinate has alternating triangular polydegree at least two, or whose U-dependence is nonlinear, while the residue ledger and Gassmann closure survive. If that fails, the remaining routes are a coupled affine modification of the explicit exceptional P1, finite-algebra descent without an ambient involution, or a new non-symmetric monodromy core.

entries.title: Cancellation arithmetic
entries.scope: Irreducibility and Galois-group questions for the cancellation parameter polynomials.

entries.title: Minimal-boundary Keller classification
entries.scope: Extract the suspension and branch labels from the intrinsic Zariski--Main package. The active cubic closure protocol has three certificates. E: local cohomology reduces the two Ext obstructions to support saturation T^[2]/T=0 and absence of closed-point cotangent torsion H_Z^0(Omega_{B/A})=0; the latter is the explicit module saturation N:I^infinity=N for I=Fitt_3(B). Their vanishing gives curvilinear fibers and flatness. P: the reduced nonproperness equation is the unique branch equation times a phantom-boundary factor, which is a unit exactly when no second unramified divisor exists; irreducibility of the nonproperness hypersurface suffices. G: single and Borel gauges, transported alternating gauges, every normalized linear cancellation, and every two-monomial cancellation are classified or excluded; a general multi-monomial two-shear equation has one possible scalar discriminant obstruction every four degrees, but the entire first bilinear obstruction vanishes identically. Vanishing E should supply a saturated Tschirnhausen flag with scalar mixed coefficient, reducing G to the proved Borel theorem and a foundational base-change rigidity problem for G=(C0,C2,C3); then P controls the sole extra target divisor. Cubic-recursion and Newton-face calculations are fallbacks, not the primary closure route. Either gateway reaches the foundational map by CM3 and MB3 or by the cubic hyperplane theorem.
supersedes: unscoped controlled-boundary suspension classification

entries.title: Wider quantization lifts and residue comparison
entries.scope: The hbar^3 obstruction vanishes uniformly. Four complete generic bounded hbar^5 periods have a zero-dimensional common scheme of exact length 44, and a fifth lowers the length to 43 without changing support. Off the replacement and degree-drop boundaries only (a,tau)=(-1/2,-3) and the cubic component 94a^3+335a^2+400a+160=0, 8tau+658a^2+1593a+976=0 remain. The rational point has an exact vertical 15-term dual functional of value one which survives the full X-Laurent lower-lift space. On the cubic component the genuine hbar^5 lift scheme has irreducible radical A^27 over Q(a), generated after free/linear elimination by six linear forms. Its explicit Q(a)-line is obstructed at hbar^7 for every parameter. On the full A^27 the 20-column hbar^7 matrix has a constant six-column pivot, every other column lies in that span, and the 401 consistency equations use only ten parameters; their ideal is the unit ideal at the two good cubic residues over GF(32003) and GF(31991). Lift this final unit identity to Q(a). For the standard 16-support period, three exact fibers show that the full 4065-variable Laurent space induces exactly the bounded 41-by-16 rank-15 constraint module; lift this equality over Q(a,tau), compute its Fitting strata, extend it to the other supports/replacement chart, formulate the coherent dual-sheaf invariant, and invariantize the formal X-adic tail. Two primitive replacement-chart periods have gcd tau^2, proving bounded obstruction there for tau!=0. On the unrestricted odd branch, exact support two, the complete residual line, every exact-support-three branch, and every nonzero-scale branch in the residual five-space are eliminated. A high-support normal-cone attack isolates an explicit projective P6 over Q(sqrt(-5)) that reaches nonzero scale at third order. The next symbol-specific task is its uniform fourth-order continuation, followed by exceptional L2 normal-cone subschemes and components outside the two generic normal charts. Eliminating this symbol would still not prove DC_2.

entries.title: Coefficient-scheme boundary gluing
entries.scope: Determine the nilpotent boundary gluing of the proved foundational coefficient scheme.

entries.title: Factorization-slice product and flexibility
entries.scope: Decide the global A^2 x SL_2 product and flexibility questions.

entries.title: Poisson-completion provenance
entries.scope: Resolve the provenance of the announced external rank-two Poisson manuscript.

entries.title: Plane-Jacobian degree frontier
entries.scope: Advance the plane-Jacobian degree frontier beyond the reproduced certified ranges by deriving exhaustive Laurent branches and nested positive branch scales, globally gluing the audited local (72,108) scale skeleton, applying the log-boundary compiler, and quotienting any matching three-band Poisson-square block by its three-component reduced classification before coefficient elimination.

entries.title: Gaussian and Image/Vanishing consequences
entries.scope: Settle GMC(2), the only unresolved real Gaussian dimension. Any counterexample must pass the quadratic, two-weight, affine-circular-source, cubic three-weight, and complete four-weight theorems: it has degree at least three, at least three rotational weights containing both signs, and nonlinear dependence on both circular coordinates; a cubic counterexample needs at least five rotational weights. The exactly-five-weight frontier is reduced to three chart orbits (ten ordinary charts on three supports), all modularly but not yet rationally excluded through order eight; the six- and seven-weight strata contain 52 ordinary charts. A positive proof must control the same genuinely shared Wick self-contractions or prove the cubic invariant-null-cone containment. Also determine which transported exceptional invariants descend under natural equivalence groups of Gaussian polynomial pairs.
